steps.title: Group and factor the polynomial
steps.content:
Rearrange and group the terms to identify a common factor:

$$(x^9 + x^8 y) + (x y^4 + y^5)$$
Factor the greatest common factor from each group:

$$x^8(x + y) + y^4(x + y)$$
Both groups contain $(x + y)$. Extract this common factor:

$$(x + y)(x^8 + y^4)$$

steps.title: Match to an answer choice
steps.content: The original polynomial factors as $(x + y)(x^8 + y^4)$, making both $(x + y)$ and $(x^8 + y^4)$ factors of the original expression. Looking at the choices, $x^8 + y^4$ appears directly as choice A — A is correct.
